name: Aguilar Reservoir and Palencian Romanesque Loop
name_original: Aguilar de Campoo, Salinas de Pisuerga y Alar del Rey – circular por el Embalse de Aguilar
description: This moderate 49.9-mile road cycling route loops around the Aguilar de Campoo Reservoir, showcasing Romanesque heritage and natural beauty.
region_intro: Road cycling around Aguilar De Campoo offers diverse landscapes, from the gentle hills surrounding the Embalse de Aguilar to demanding climbs towards the Cantabrian Mountains. The region is characterized by its significant Romanesque heritage, with routes often connecting historical sites and natural features. Cyclists can explore varied terrain, including areas near the Palentina Mountain and the Las Loras UNESCO World Geopark. This mix provides options for both leisure riders and those seeking more challenging ascents. Best road cycling routes around Aguilar De Campoo The most popular road cycling route is Pedaling the Palencian Romanesque, a 45.0 miles (72.4 km) trail that takes 3 hours 8 minutes to complete. This route showcases the scenic Embalse de Aguilar and the region's historical Romanesque architecture. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Salinas de Pisuerga from Aguilar de Campoo – circular by the Embalse de Aguilar, a moderate 20.3 miles (32.6 km) path. This circular route offers views of the reservoir and surrounding countryside. Local road cyclists also love the Aguilar de Campoo, Embalse de Aguilar, El Alto de Bardal – circular route, a 38.6 miles (62.2 km) trail leading through the Embalse de Aguilar and El Alto de Bardal, often completed in about 3 hours 19 minutes. Road cycling around Aguilar De Campoo is defined by the Embalse de Aguilar, the Palentina Mountain, and numerous Romanesque sites. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate rides around the reservoir to routes with significant elevation gains. The routes in Aguilar De Campoo are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.2 stars from over 14 reviews. More than 600 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Aguilar De Campoo's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Aguilar De Campoo?
A: Aguilar De Campoo offers a wide selection of road cycling routes, with over 45 options available. These routes cater to various skill levels, from moderate rides to more challenging ascents.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on road cycling routes in Aguilar De Campoo?
A: The terrain around Aguilar De Campoo is quite diverse. You'll find gentle hills surrounding the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1154697640/salinas-de-pisuerga-from-aguilar-de-campoo-circular-by-the-embalse-de-aguilar" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Embalse de Aguilar&lt;/a&gt;, as well as demanding climbs towards the Cantabrian Mountains and the Picos de Europa. Routes can incorporate old mining roads and nature trails, offering a varied experience.

Q: When is the best time of year for road cycling in Aguilar De Campoo?
A: The months of April and June are particularly popular for road cycling in Aguilar De Campoo, offering pleasant weather conditions for exploring the region's diverse landscapes.

Q: Are there circular road cycling routes available in Aguilar De Campoo?
A: Yes, there are several excellent circular road cycling routes. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1154697640/salinas-de-pisuerga-from-aguilar-de-campoo-circular-by-the-embalse-de-aguilar" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Salinas de Pisuerga from Aguilar de Campoo – circular by the Embalse de Aguilar&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 32.6 km path, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1005120068/aguilar-de-campoo-embalse-de-aguilar-el-alto-de-bardal-circular-route" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Aguilar de Campoo, Embalse de Aguilar, El Alto de Bardal – circular route&lt;/a&gt; covers 62.2 km.

Q: Can I combine road cycling with exploring historical sites in Aguilar De Campoo?
A: Absolutely. Aguilar De Campoo is rich in Romanesque heritage. Many routes, such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1298680521/pedaling-the-palencian-romanesque" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pedaling the Palencian Romanesque&lt;/a&gt;, connect historical sites like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4848732" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monastery of Santa María la Real&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5786243" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Salinas de Pisuerga Bridge&lt;/a&gt;, allowing you to combine sport with cultural exploration.

Q: What natural features or viewpoints can I see while road cycling in Aguilar De Campoo?
A: You'll encounter stunning natural beauty, including the picturesque &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1298673397/aguilar-reservoir-and-palencian-romanesque-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Aguilar Reservoir (Embalse de Aguilar)&lt;/a&gt;. Routes also offer views of impressive geological formations like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4413098" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peña Mesa&lt;/a&gt; within the Las Tuerces Natural Area, and the dramatic backdrop of the Palentina Mountain and the broader Cantabrian Mountains.

Q: Are there challenging road cycling routes for experienced cyclists?
A: Yes, for experienced cyclists seeking a challenge, the region offers routes with significant elevation gains. Options extend towards the Picos de Europa, such as the 105.2 km "Aguilar - Fuente Dé" with a 1,430 m ascent, or the 168.9 km "E3 Aguilar - Chivo" with a 2,423 m ascent, providing demanding climbs.

Q: What do other road cyclists appreciate most about the routes in Aguilar De Campoo?
A: The road cycling routes in Aguilar De Campoo are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.2 stars. Cyclists often praise the diverse landscapes, the combination of natural beauty with historical sites, and the variety of routes suitable for different abilities.

Q: What are some notable attractions near the road cycling routes?
A: Beyond the cycling, you can explore attractions like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4848732" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monastery of Santa María la Real&lt;/a&gt;, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5786243" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Salinas de Pisuerga Bridge&lt;/a&gt;, and the distinctive geological formation of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4413098" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peña Mesa&lt;/a&gt;. The area is also a gateway to the Montaña Palentina Natural Park and the Las Loras UNESCO World Geopark.

Q: What are the typical lengths of road cycling routes in the area?
A: Road cycling routes around Aguilar De Campoo vary significantly in length. You can find moderate paths around 30-40 km, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1154697640/salinas-de-pisuerga-from-aguilar-de-campoo-circular-by-the-embalse-de-aguilar" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Salinas de Pisuerga circular route&lt;/a&gt; (32.6 km), up to longer, more challenging rides exceeding 100 km, like the "Aguilar - Fuente Dé" route.

Q: Are there places to stop for refreshments along the road cycling routes?
A: While specific cafes aren't listed for every route, Aguilar De Campoo and the surrounding villages offer various establishments where you can find refreshments. Planning your route to pass through these towns will ensure opportunities for breaks.

Q: Is road cycling possible in Aguilar De Campoo during winter?
A: While April and June are highlighted as popular cycling months, the region's proximity to the Cantabrian Mountains means winter conditions can be colder and potentially include snow, especially on higher elevation routes. It's advisable to check local weather forecasts and route conditions if planning to cycle during winter.
tips: There are many things to see in Aguilar de Campoo. Apart from having several biscuit factories, it is also the town of the Romanesque, of the manor houses, shields and coats of arms. In fact, this town and its surroundings are home to the highest concentration of Romanesque monuments in all of Europe.

Locality of the Palencia Mountain whose name comes, as they say, because it is located in a salty soil.
It has a parish church (San Pelayo) from the 16th century with a large bell tower.
It is worth visiting the medieval bridge that crosses the waters of the Pisuerga river, built in the 16th century.
Small Hermitage of Santa Eulalia from the 12th century built on top of a hill near the town of Barrio de Santa María.

The power of water, the small town of Alar del Rey is literally surrounded by water. The beginning of the Canal de Castilla, an industrial attempt to boost trade by taking advantage of the power of the river during the 18th century. Today, it is a cultural and sports center with the canal a site of cultural importance.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this road cycling route?
faqs.answer: The route is approximately 80.3 kilometers long and is generally categorized as a moderate road ride. Cyclists with good fitness levels can expect to complete it in about 3.5 hours of active cycling time, though this can vary depending on pace and stops at points of interest.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like for road cycling on this route?
faqs.answer: The trail primarily uses well-paved surfaces, making it ideal for road bikes. While the region has varied terrain, this specific route is predominantly on good quality tarmac, offering a smooth ride through diverse landscapes.

faqs.question: Where can I park to start the circular route?
faqs.answer: The route is circular, so you can start from various points. Aguilar de Campoo is a common starting point, offering parking options within the town. Other villages along the route, such as Salinas de Pisuerga or Alar del Rey, may also have suitable parking areas.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the way?
faqs.answer: This route is rich in both natural beauty and historical sites. You'll pass by the impressive Aguilar de Campoo Castle and Romanesque Monuments, the scenic Salinas de Pisuerga Bridge, and the historic Hermitage of Santa Eulalia of the Barrio de Santa María. Another notable feature is the Dock and Locks of the Canal de Castilla in Alar del Rey, showcasing engineering heritage. The Aguilar de Campoo Reservoir itself is a central scenic highlight.

faqs.question: Is this road cycling route suitable for beginners or families?
faqs.answer: This route is classified as 'moderate' due to its length (80.3 km) and elevation gain (around 700 meters). It requires a good level of fitness and is generally more suitable for experienced road cyclists. While families with older, experienced children might manage, it's not typically recommended for beginners or very young children.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The best time to cycle this route is typically during the spring (April to June) and early autumn (September to October). During these months, the weather is generally milder and more pleasant for cycling, avoiding the intense heat of summer and the colder, potentially wetter conditions of winter.

faqs.question: Are dogs allowed on this cycling route?
faqs.answer: As this is a road cycling route primarily on public roads and through villages, dogs are generally permitted, especially if kept on a leash. However, always be mindful of local regulations in towns and natural areas, and ensure your dog is comfortable with the distance and pace if accompanying you.

faqs.question: Do I need any special permits or pay entrance fees for this route?
faqs.answer: No, you do not need any special permits or pay entrance fees to cycle this specific route. It utilizes public roads and passes through towns and natural areas that do not require permits for access. However, any specific attractions you choose to visit along the way (e.g., museums, castles) may have their own entrance fees.

faqs.question: Does the route pass through or near the Montaña Palentina Natural Park, and are there any regulations to be aware of?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route is situated at the entrance to the Montaña Palentina Natural Park and borders the Las Loras UNESCO World Geopark. While the cycling route itself primarily follows roads, you are in an area of significant natural beauty. There are no specific permits required for cycling on the public roads within or near the park's boundaries. However, if you venture off the main route into the natural park for other activities, be aware of general park regulations regarding waste, fire, and respecting wildlife.

faqs.question: Are there cafes, restaurants, or accommodation options along the route?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route passes through several towns and villages, including Aguilar de Campoo, Salinas de Pisuerga, and Alar del Rey. These places offer various amenities such as cafes, restaurants, and accommodation options, making it easy to refuel or find a place to stay overnight.

faqs.question: Does this route intersect with any other well-known trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route shares sections with several other paths. Notably, it intersects with parts of the Camino del Besaya: Palencia, the Camino Olvidado (Matamorisca-Puente Almuhey), the Románico Palentino, and the Camino Natural del Románico Palentino. Ruta del Carbón de cok. Etapa 1. You'll also find it briefly overlaps with the Canal de Castilla (Ramal Norte-Sur) and the GR-89 BTT Canal de Castilla (Ramal Norte Etapa Alar - Frómista).
